name: Supercritical Wing Design
summary: A transonic wing-design approach that delays shock formation and reduces wave drag by shaping the upper surface and pressure recovery.
whenToUseIt: Use it when designing transport-aircraft wings for high-subsonic cruise where transonic drag rise threatens fuel burn and buffet margins.
steps: Set the cruise Mach number, altitude, and lift coefficient targets.
Choose aerofoil families that flatten the upper-surface pressure peak.
Manage shock location and strength across the span.
Check drag divergence, buffet onset, and structural implications.
Refine the wing-body integration to preserve the transonic benefit.
example: Modern long-range airliners rely on supercritical sections to cruise near Mach 0.85 without paying an unacceptable wave-drag penalty, which directly improves airline economics.
